description_long: This mosque features a grand central structure with a large, ribbed dome flanked by two smaller domes, all with a slightly greenish patina. The main building is surrounded by a series of arched colonnades and semi-circular porticos, giving it an open and inviting appearance. Two tall, slender minarets with pointed conical roofs rise dramatically above the main dome, each topped with a small finial. A separate, cylindrical minaret with spiral staircases and open balconies stands adjacent to the main structure, adding vertical emphasis. The surfaces are predominantly a pale, marble-like stone with subtle weathering, accented by greenish-blue domes and roof elements. The overall design is ornate yet harmonious, with a blend of traditional Islamic architectural motifs and a slightly fantastical interpretation.
description: A grand mosque with a central ribbed dome, two tall minarets, and arched colonnades, featuring pale stone and greenish domes. A separate spiral minaret adds vertical emphasis and architectural interest.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance, central dome, two tall minarets, and the adjacent spiral minaret with open balconies.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the layered domes, the rear of the spiral minaret, and the intricate arrangement of arched porticos.
description_view_2: The rear view emphasizes the height of the twin minarets and the symmetry of the main structure, with the spiral minaret positioned to the side.
description_view_3: This angle showcases the spiral staircase of the separate minaret and the arrangement of domes and colonnades from the back corner.
attribute_dome: Three domes are visible: one large central ribbed dome and two smaller domes, all with a greenish patina and rounded shape, positioned atop the main structure.
attribute_minaret: Three minarets are visible: two tall, slender minarets with pointed conical roofs attached to the main building, and one separate cylindrical minaret with spiral staircases and open balconies, all taller than the main dome.
attribute_islamic_geometric_patterns: None visible; surfaces appear plain with no discernible geometric patterns.
attribute_arabic_calligraphy: None visible; no Arabic calligraphy can be seen on domes, entrances, or panels.
attribute_architectural_style: The mosque exhibits a blend of Ottoman and fantastical Islamic architectural styles, characterized by ribbed domes, tall slender minarets, arched colonnades, and a prominent spiral minaret.
